reason: Frontend spec entry — lists all conventions; this task lives in web/ frontend layer.
Feature-first layout; this task adds web/src/features/logs/{components,hooks,api,routes} with two routes (events, trends).

Stateful render branches, no-color-only state semantics, mandatory unauthorized vs empty vs error separation. Log surface depends on this for the masked-cell rendering and targetAuthorized=false red-badge requirement.
Domain hook naming (use-security-log-events, use-attack-trends); query-key conventions; URL-state filter encoding with mandatory assetGroupId.
URL state for filter/sort/page/since/until; server state via TanStack Query; no copying redacted/masked event fields to global state; tab navigation preserves shared filters.
Branded ID aliases for EventId/SourceId/AssetGroupId; runtime zod parse with redactedFields: z.array(z.string()) preservation; forbidden any.
UI forbidden patterns — sensitive fields rendered as plain strings, masked null treated as empty, missing dashboard text-summary, color-only attack signaling — all relevant here.
Frontend mirrors backend zod contracts; log mirror must preserve all enums (logType / parserFormat / redactionStatus / parseStatus / securityLogSeverity) verbatim.
Reuse <StatusBadge>/<UnauthorizedState>/<EmptyState>/<ErrorState> from console + vuln + mail; new <MaskedCell /> component to be reusable for weak-password surface.

Platform PRD §7.7 log analysis (no SIEM, no auto-block, no auto-scope-expand), §8 data contract (180-day events / 1-year aggregates), §3 viewer/auditor permission boundaries, dashboard text-summary philosophy.
Parent MVP PRD: wave order, 180-day retention global constraint, frontend stack constraints.
Tracker parent PRD: shared contract mirror policy, URL state spec, sensitive display rules, role gating, suggested implementation order.
Sibling task that builds App / router / Providers / MSW worker / fetch helper / ActorProvider; this task depends on its shells and reuses its shared components.
Sibling task that established the 4-domain shared list pattern (URL state, viewer route guard, MSW handler-side query/grouping); log surface inherits these conventions.
Sibling task that established the no-raw-body display constraint and explicit-edge-case banner pattern; log surface adopts the same no-raw-payload constraint.
This task's own PRD with locked decisions, two pages, hooks, MSW handlers, ADR-lite, R1–R12, AC.

Source of truth for logType / parserFormat / redactionStatus / parseStatus / securityLogSeverity enums, normalizedSecurityLogEventSchema, attackTrendBucketSchema; web mirror copies field names/enums verbatim.
SecurityLogEventQuery and AttackTrendQuery dimensions (assetGroupId mandatory) define the exact filter surface the MSW handlers must support and what the URL state guard must enforce.
Shared severity vocabulary and permissionPoints (raw_evidence:view) used for route-level gating.
API error envelope shape that log MSW handlers must emit.
ActorContext / permissionPoints consumed by route-level RequireRawEvidence guard and assetGroup membership check.
